title: Set Up Git Identity and Make First Commit

steps.title: Open a Terminal
steps.instruction: Open a terminal. On Windows you can use Command Prompt, PowerShell, Git Bash or the integrated terminal in VS Code. On Mac or Linux, just open your regular terminal application.

steps.title: Configure Global Username
steps.instruction: Configure the global username with 'git config --global user.name' and your name in double quotes. For example, 'git config --global user.name "John Doe"'.

steps.title: Configure Email Address
steps.instruction: Set the email address with 'git config --global user.email' and your email address in double quotes. For example, 'git config --global user.email "john.doe@example.com"'.

steps.title: Verify Git Configuration
steps.instruction: Verify both settings were saved correctly with 'git config --global --list'.

steps.title: Create a New Project Directory
steps.instruction: Create a new directory for your project with 'mkdir myproject'.

steps.title: Navigate to Project Directory
steps.instruction: Navigate into the project directory with 'cd myproject'.

steps.title: Initialize a Git Repository
steps.instruction: Initialize a git repository in this folder with 'git init'.

steps.title: Create a File to Commit
steps.instruction: Create a simple file to commit with 'echo hello world > readme.md'.

steps.title: Check Repository Status
steps.instruction: Check the status of the repository with 'git status'.

steps.title: Add File to Repository
steps.instruction: Add the file to the repository with 'git add readme.md'.

steps.title: Verify File Added
steps.instruction: Verify the file was added with 'git status'.

steps.title: Make the First Commit
steps.instruction: Make the commit with 'git commit -m "initial commit"'.

steps.title: Verify Commit History
steps.instruction: Verify the commit history with 'git log'.
